headline: Exxon Soars. Why Oil Stocks Could Go Even Higher.
summary: Exxon Mobil and other oil stocks were soaring early Monday amid a crude price surge linked to tit-for-tat attacks between the U.S., Israel and Iran.  Shares in the major oil companies were rallying in premarket trading.  Exxon  was up 4.6% to $159.49, while  Chevron  was trading 3.9% higher at $194.05.

content.title: Exxon Soars. Why Oil Stocks Could Go Even Higher.
content.summary: Exxon Mobil and other oil stocks were soaring early Monday amid a crude price surge linked to tit-for-tat attacks between the U.S., Israel and Iran.  Shares in the major oil companies were rallying in premarket trading.  Exxon  was up 4.6% to $159.49, while  Chevron  was trading 3.9% higher at $194.05.

headline: Stock Index Futures Plunge Amid Middle East Conflict
summary: March S&P 500 E-Mini futures (ESH26) are down -1.05%, and March Nasdaq 100 E-Mini futures (NQH26) are down -1.44% this morning as sentiment took a hit after the U.S. and Israel exchanged deadly strikes with Iran across the Middle East.

content.title: Stock Index Futures Plunge Amid Middle East Conflict

headline: Dow Jones leads modest retreat as Wall Street aloof to Iran war
summary: 10am: Wall Street starts with modest falls Initial trading in New York was less pessimistic than in Europe, with losses for the main US indices considerably smaller in initial trades. The Dow Jones is the worst, down 0.65%, followed by the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq Composite both falling...

content.title: Dow Jones leads modest retreat as Wall Street aloof to Iran war
content.summary: 10am: Wall Street starts with modest falls Initial trading in New York was less pessimistic than in Europe, with losses for the main US indices considerably smaller in initial trades. The Dow Jones is the worst, down 0.65%, followed by the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq Composite both falling...

headline: Exxon, Chevron, and Other Energy Stocks Rise. Why Shares Can Go Even Higher.
summary: Exxon Mobil and other oil stocks were rising early Monday amid a  crude price  surge linked to tit-for-tat attacks between the U.S., Israel, and Iran.  Shares in the major oil companies were climbing in early trading.  Exxon  was up 2%, while  Chevron  was trading 1.1% higher.

headline: Energy stocks rise on oil prices, BlackRock consortium to acquire AES
summary: Market Catalysts Host Julie Hyman tracks several of the day's top trending stock tickers, including energy stocks moving higher on spiking oil prices (CL=F, BZ=F), shares of Palantir Technologies (PLTR) getting a boost amid escalations in the Middle East after the US bombing of Iran over the weekend, and BlackRock-owned (BLK) Global Infrastructure Partners leading a consortium to acquire AES (AES) for $10.7 billion. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Catalysts.

content.title: Energy stocks rise on oil prices, BlackRock consortium to acquire AES
content.description:
Market Catalysts Host Julie Hyman tracks several of the day's top trending stock tickers, including energy stocks moving higher on spiking oil prices (CL=F, BZ=F), shares of Palantir Technologies (PLTR) getting a boost amid escalations in the Middle East after the US bombing of Iran over the weekend, and BlackRock-owned (BLK) Global Infrastructure Partners leading a consortium to acquire AES (AES) for $10.7 billion.

To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Catalysts.

headline: Chevron, Exxon price targets raised at BofA on increased oil risk premium

headline: U.S. Energy Shares Jump After Iran War Intensifies
summary: Fears that the war on Iran could upend global markets sent share prices climbing across U.S. oil majors, Texas frackers, Appalachian gas producers, Gulf Coast energy exporters and more.  Liquefied natural-gas exporter Venture Global recently jumped 18%, while Cheniere climbed 5%.

content.title: U.S. Energy Shares Jump After Iran War Intensifies
content.summary: Fears that the war on Iran could upend global markets sent share prices climbing across U.S. oil majors, Texas frackers, Appalachian gas producers, Gulf Coast energy exporters and more.  Liquefied natural-gas exporter Venture Global recently jumped 18%, while Cheniere climbed 5%.

headline: Gas prices will soar 'very quickly' as Iran conflict boosts oil
summary: Oil (CL=F, BZ=F) prices are surging as the US's conflict with Iran escalates. GasBuddy head of petroleum analysis Patrick De Haan and Yahoo Finance Breaking Business News Reporter Jake Conley join Market Catalysts host Julie Hyman to discuss what higher oil prices mean for gas prices and consumers. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Catalysts.
